messages.content: Build a sequential user-loading pipeline in Effect-TS v3 using Effect.gen with yield* — given a userId, fetch the user, then fetch their orders, and return both.
What's the Effect v3 way to sequence three dependent effects inside a generator? Show a minimal example.
Show me the Effect v3 equivalent of `Effect.gen(function*(_) { const x = yield* _(eff) })` from v2. Brief example.
Convert this Effect-TS v2 generator to v3 idioms: `Effect.gen(function*($) { const u = yield* $(getUser()); const o = yield* $(getOrders(u.id)); return o })`
Explain briefly how Effect.gen with yield* works in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation that reads a row from a database and publishes an event.
Write an Effect-TS v3 program that calculates an order total using Effect.gen. Constraints: strict types, no any, named tagged errors, no flatMap chains.
Refactor this Effect.flatMap chain to use Effect.gen: `pipe(readA, Effect.flatMap((a) => readB(a)), Effect.flatMap((b) => readC(b)))`
Implement a typed retry-once-then-fallback flow in Effect v3 with strict types — no any, all errors and values typed, using Effect.gen.
Add a cache-lookup short-circuit to this Effect v3 pipeline: `Effect.gen(function* () { const u = yield* fetchUser(id); return u })`
What's the idiomatic Effect v3 way to loop over a list and collect results inside a generator? Minimal example.
Build a checkout flow in Effect-TS v3 using Effect.gen with typed events and context — reserve inventory, charge card, emit receipt, all sequential.
Fix this Effect v2 fragment to v3 idioms: `Effect.gen(function*(_) { return yield* _(Effect.succeed(1)) })`
Explain briefly how typed errors flow through Effect.gen in v3, then show a working implementation that parses JSON and validates a schema in one generator.
Add error accumulation to this v3 generator: `Effect.gen(function* () { const a = yield* op1(); const b = yield* op2(a); return b })`
Implement a multi-step onboarding effect in Effect v3 with strict types — send welcome email, create profile, enroll in default plan — via Effect.gen.
What's the Effect v3 way to interleave logging into a sequential generator without a separate flatMap step? Minimal example.
Refactor this v3 code to use Effect.gen instead of flatMap: `readUser(id).pipe(Effect.flatMap((u) => readPosts(u.id).pipe(Effect.map((p) => ({ user: u, posts: p })))))`
Build a poll-until-ready effect in Effect v3 using Effect.gen — fetch status, return when done, otherwise sleep and recurse.
Write an Effect v3 generator that parses a header line then N body lines from an async reader. Constraints: strict types, no any, no flatMap chains.
Build a service-graph for a todo app in Effect-TS v3 using Layer composition — Logger, TodoStore (depends on Logger), and a program that uses both.
What's the Effect v3 way to merge two independent layers into one? Show a minimal example.
Show me the Effect v3 equivalent of providing multiple services to a program. Brief example.
Fix this Effect v2 layer wiring to v3: `Layer.provide(Inner)(Outer)` and `Effect.provideLayer(program, L)`
Explain briefly how Layer composition works in Effect v3, then show a working implementation for an app with Config, Db (needs Config), and Cache (needs Config).
Write an Effect v3 layer graph that wires an HTTP client depending on a Logger and a RateLimiter. Constraints: strict types, no any, include a comment showing the dependency direction.
Add a test layer override for Logger to this wiring: `const AppLayer = Layer.merge(LoggerLive, StoreLive)`
What's the difference between Layer.succeed and Layer.effect in Effect v3? Show a minimal example with both.
Convert this Effect v2 layer wiring to v3: `pipe(MainLive, Layer.provide(LoggerLive), Layer.provide(ConfigLive))`
Refactor this layer code to use Layer.mergeAll for readability: `Layer.merge(Layer.merge(A, B), Layer.merge(C, D))`
Explain briefly how Layer.effect builds a service that itself needs other services, then show a working typed example.
Build an Effect v3 layer stack for a worker: JobQueue (needs Db), Db (needs Config), Config (leaf). Show the full composition.
What's the Effect v3 way to swap a real DB layer for an in-memory test double? Minimal example.
Fix this Effect v2 fragment to v3: `Effect.provideLayer(program, Layer.mergeAll(A, B, C))`
Refactor this v3 layer graph to be easier to read: `Effect.provide(program, Layer.provide(Layer.provide(Main, L1), L2))`
Implement a metrics-instrumented layer stack in Effect v3 with strict types — Counter service, Timer service, and a Metrics service that uses both.
Write an Effect v3 layer for an auth service that reads from Config. Constraints: use Layer.effect, include an Effect.provide call at the edge, show dependency direction in a comment.
Add a Health service to this Effect v3 layer graph that depends on Db: `const AppLayer = Layer.provide(DbLive, ConfigLive)`
Build a tiny Effect v3 app using Layer composition: Env service (pure), Tracer service (needs Env), program that uses both — show Effect.provide at the edge.
What's the Effect v3 way to expose a service transitively after Layer.provide has consumed it? Minimal example.
Explain briefly how Layer.merge differs from Layer.provide in Effect v3, then show a working example that uses both.
